purpose: Freeze the module-owned executable surface baseline for the OPL brand modules so L4 is proven by each module family, not only by the aggregate registry.
machine_boundary: This contract defines module-level object models, native CLI families, App/read-model descriptors, validation and doctor gates, and false-authority boundaries. It does not claim domain ready, production ready, quality verdict, artifact authority, owner receipt, typed blocker, App release truth, or body storage authority.

modules.notes: Charter owns OPL module governance, terms, authority, and decision lifecycle surfaces; it does not own runtime or domain truth.
Atlas owns refs-only catalogs for agents, descriptors, graphs, lifecycle, generated interfaces, and domain capability registry metadata; it does not execute cataloged actions or own domain authority.
Workspace owns workspace topology, binding, index, and project/stage artifact location surfaces; it does not own domain truth or artifact bodies.

Pack owns declarative Domain Pack shape, domain capability registry ABI, Agent Tool Arsenal / Capability Invocation ABI, authority ABI refs, pack compiler output, generated surface boundary, capability execution view, agent operational card, ToolResultEnvelope shape, and the three-layer capability invocation lifecycle policy. Pack contracts are generation and validation material; the ordinary Agent path consumes execution views, operational cards, result envelopes, and lifecycle projections, not raw MAS or domain contract details. Pack does not own domain truth, artifact body, owner receipt, typed blocker, quality/export verdict authority, or current-owner authorization.

Stagecraft owns stage grammar, capability use policy, StageRun shape, role artifact interfaces, and receipt/blocker binding surfaces; it does not sign domain receipts or turn optional capability refs into launch hard gates.

Runway owns the control-loop runtime module: runtime intent admission, queue/readiness reconciliation, provider attempt orchestration refs, handoff gates, and recovery repair. The executable read surfaces are status/inspect/interfaces/validate/doctor plus readiness/reconcile/handoff-gates/recovery-repair/control-loop status. recovery-repair exposes provider repair, worker restart guard, and a refs-only attempt_repair_queue for blocked/failed attempt operator review. Temporal not configured, service down, worker not ready, or scheduler missing is provider_not_ready/OPL repair action, not domain readiness. Temporal is durable substrate, worker supervision is process liveness, and the Progress Reconciler is the semantic/execution loop projection; Runway does not write domain truth, sign owner receipts, create typed blockers, convert provider completion into domain readiness, or close L5 production long-soak.
Ledger owns refs-only evidence, receipt refs, blocker refs, lineage, restore, and index audit surfaces; it does not store artifact or memory bodies and does not turn refs into owner authority.

Console owns OPL-produced current_owner_delta default read models, capability execution view projections, agent operational card projections, ToolResultEnvelope projections, App/operator projections, and safe action descriptors. Console ordinary paths consume Pack-produced execution views, operational cards, and result envelopes; they do not consume raw MAS contract details, sign owner answers, own domain truth, or hold App release truth.
Foundry Lab owns agent blueprint, conformance, work-order, canary, and promotion/rollback improvement surfaces; domain owner acceptance remains external.

Connect owns generated descriptors, capability execution view descriptors, agent operational card descriptors, ToolResultEnvelope descriptors, package/install surfaces, skill sync, channel, and drift projections. Connect exports ordinary Agent consumption descriptors derived from Pack contracts; it does not export raw MAS contract details, reinterpret domain semantics, or treat transport success as semantic authority.
